title: Distribution control messages — LINK, EXIT, MONITOR, SEND
purpose: Beyond message send, distribution uses control messages for process lifecycle: LINK establishes cross-node links, EXIT propagates exit signals, MONITOR/DEMONITOR manage cross-node monitors, and EXIT2 sends explicit exit signals. These complete the supervision protocol across node boundaries.
task: Implement all distribution control message types for process lifecycle.

requirements.title: Control message dispatch table
requirements.spec: Define the control message type enum and dispatch incoming control messages to appropriate handlers.
acceptance: ControlOp enum: Send(2), RegSend(6), Link(1), Exit(3), Unlink(4), MonitorP(19), DemonitorP(20), MonitorPExit(21), Exit2(8), SpawnRequest(29), SpawnReply(31)
Incoming control messages dispatched by op code
Unknown op codes logged and ignored (forward compatibility)
Test: dispatch each known op code to correct handler

requirements.title: LINK and UNLINK control messages
requirements.spec: Implement cross-node link establishment and removal.
acceptance: Outbound: when link(LocalPid, RemotePid) is called, send LINK control to remote node
Inbound: receiving LINK control establishes the link on the local side
Test: link two processes on different nodes, verify bidirectional

requirements.title: EXIT and EXIT2 control messages
requirements.spec: Propagate exit signals across node boundaries.
acceptance: When a linked process exits, EXIT control sent to remote linked processes
EXIT carries the exit reason as an ETF-encoded term
EXIT2 (explicit exit/2 to remote PID) sends the signal
Remote node delivers the exit signal to the target process (same as local exit signal)
Test: process on A linked to process on B, A exits, B receives exit signal

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement SEND_SENDER (OTP 21+ optimization) — use basic SEND
SHALL NOT implement PAYLOAD_EXIT (exit with large reason) — use basic EXIT
SHALL handle all control messages even if some handlers are stubs initially
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
